title: cornaceae
intro: The cornaceae rank, also known as the Dogwood family, refers to a grouping of flowering plants that share distinct characteristics. This family includes approximately 50 genera and 800 species, with the majority being shrubs or small trees. The cornaceae family is known for its showy flowers, often with four or five petals, and its ability to thrive in a variety of environments. These plants are commonly found in temperate and subtropical regions, and some species are highly valued for their ornamental and edible properties.
